sourceMovie.title: Forrest Gump
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Forrest Gump works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: As Good as It Gets
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: As Good as It Gets (1997) belongs to the same conversation as Forrest Gump — both anchor their comedy in drama emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Apartment
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Forrest Gump's comedy stayed with you, The Apartment (1960) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The drama through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: About a Boy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Forrest Gump excels in comedy, About a Boy (2002) doubles down on drama to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Ruby Sparks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ruby Sparks (2012) is the natural next step after Forrest Gump: same comedy DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and drama elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Junkyard Dog
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Forrest Gump who respond to its comedy will find Junkyard Dog (2023) immediately familiar. Both films share a drama sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Brokeback Mountain
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Forrest Gump fans keep landing on Brokeback Mountain (2005) — the drama is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon draws from the same drama well as Forrest Gump, channeling romance in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2000 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Holdovers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Holdovers (2023) captures the comedy appeal of Forrest Gump without simply imitating it. The drama streak running through both films is what puts The Holdovers at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Breaking the Waves
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama appeal of Forrest Gump — that bittersweet edge, the romance — translates cleanly into Breaking the Waves (1996). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Two Lovers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Two Lovers (2008) clearly understood what makes drama work — the same quality that keeps Forrest Gump rewatchable. Expect the same bittersweet rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

faq.question: What should I watch after Forrest Gump?
faq.answer: Try As Good as It Gets, The Apartment, About a Boy—each shares DNA with Forrest Gump while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Forrest Gump?
faq.answer: Forrest Gump (1994) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Forrest Gump worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
